name: Peppercorn Pork
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the peppercorns, mustard, horseradish, lemon peel, and salt.
2. Spread the mixture evenly on top of and around the pork roast.
3. Place a metal rack in the bottom of the slow cooker.
4. Pour the cider into the slow cooker.
5. Place the coated pork roast on the rack inside the slow cooker.
6. Cover and cook on LOW for 9-10 hours.
7. After cooking on LOW, turn the heat to HIGH.
8. Remove the pork and rack; cover and keep warm.
9. In a small bowl, combine the cold water and cornstarch; stir until smooth.
10. Add the mixture to the drippings in the pot.
11. Cook on HIGH for 20-30 minutes or until thickened, stirring occasionally.
12. Slice the roast and garnish with apple wedges.
13. Serve pork with sauce.
